response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web-platform engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position has a hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency, which the candidate does not possess — his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond this disqualifying gap, his career is almost entirely web, cloud, and TypeScript/Node.js work with no demonstrable background in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDKs. His brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a thesis project does not constitute the depth of embedded C and kernel-level Linux work this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple stacks, demonstrating technical adaptability
Hands-on C and C++ background from National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and a Linux-based Wave Field Synthesis thesis project
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and Linux familiarity across multiple roles
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this position
Zero experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch firmware, or Layer 2/3 protocols)
Career trajectory is entirely web/cloud/full-stack; no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, chip-vendor SDKs, or embedded device hardware control

key_factors.factor: Long software career with some C/Linux exposure
